notes.note: Marcus Junius Brutus, the future tyrannicide and dedicatee of Cicero's De Finibus, then preparing to leave Rome on a sudden long journey. Cicero is weighing whether to keep him company at Rome or Tusculum.

The dialogue Cicero was dedicating to the polymath M. Terentius Varro --- the revised Academica --- in copyists' hands and nearly finished. Cicero fretted over the dedication throughout these letters.

A retentio: a clause by which a debtor or buyer holds back part of a sum pending some condition. Cicero, advised by the jurist Trebatius, is pressing Atticus to settle an account without letting the other party exploit such a hold.
Gaius Trebatius Testa, the jurist and friend of Cicero (dedicatee of the Topica), here cited for his legal view on the withholding-clause.
Scaeva, invoked as the type of the relentless creditor: Cicero tells Atticus to deal with Polla as ruthlessly as that Scaeva would. The English renders the name loosely as 'Scaevola.'
